---
title: "Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)"
subtitle: "CMS Hospital Quality — Readmission Modeling Project"
Author : Waldo Ketonou
output: html_document
---

# Overview

This EDA follows the project narrative and focuses on understanding:

- The target variable (**readmission_index**, ERR proxy)  
- The three engineered CMS quality domains  
- Their relationships with readmission performance  
- Regional variation across U.S. Census regions  

All plots referenced below are saved in:
visuals/r_plots/eda/

The full reproducible code is located in:
r/01_healthcare_aligned_eda.R

---

# 1. Target Variable: Readmission Index (ERR Proxy)

## 1.1 Missingness

CMS only reports ERR for hospitals with sufficient qualifying cases.  
As expected, a large portion of hospitals do not have ERR values.

**Key insight:**  
Missingness is **domain‑driven**, not a data quality issue.

## 1.2 Distribution

```{r echo=FALSE, out.width="70%"}
knitr::include_graphics("visuals/r_plots/eda/readmission_index_hist.png")
```
• 	Distribution centered at 0.999, matching CMS national patterns
• 	Tight spread (SD ≈ 0.058)
• 	Suitable for linear modeling
##  Outlier Check
```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("visuals/r_plots/eda/readmission_index_boxplot.png")
```
• 	Mild outliers represent real hospitals
• 	No structural issues
## 1.4 Hospitals With vs Without ERR
• 	Has ERR: 2,326
• 	Missing ERR: 3,100
Only hospitals with ERR will be used for modeling.
## 2. Quality Domain Exploration
The project uses three engineered CMS quality domains:
• 	mortality_index
• 	patient_experience_score (HCAHPS)
• 	timely_effective_care_score (TEC)
Each domain was evaluated for missingness, distribution, and interpretability.
## 2.1 Mortality Index
```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("visuals/r_plots/eda/mortality_index_hist.png")
```
• 	Moderate missingness
• 	Stable distribution
• 	Known positive association with readmissions
## 2.2 Patient Experience (HCAHPS)
```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("visuals/r_plots/eda/patient_experience_hist.png")
```
- Wide variation
- Reflects operational performance differences

## 3. Relationship Analysis
This section answers the narrative’s core question:
Which quality factors most influence hospital readmission performance?

Scatterplots with linear trendlines were used to evaluate relationships.

## 3.1 Mortality Index → Readmission Index
```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("visuals/r_plots/eda/mortality_vs_readmission.png")
```
- Positive association
- Higher mortality correlates with higher readmissions

## 3.2 Patient Experience → Readmission Index
```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("visuals/r_plots/eda/patient_experience_vs_readmission.png")
```
- Strong negative association
- Better patient experience correlates with lower readmissions

## 3.3 TEC Score → Readmission Index
```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("visuals/r_plots/eda/tec_vs_readmission.png")
```
- Weak but present negative association

## 4. Regional Variation
Regions were assigned using U.S. Census definitions.
Regional variation supports the narrative’s claim that improvement opportunities differ geographically.

## 4.1 Readmission Index by Region
```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("visuals/r_plots/eda/readmission_by_region.png")
```
- The South shows the highest readmission levels

## 4.2 Mortality Index by Region
```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("visuals/r_plots/eda/mortality_by_region.png")
```
- Mortality is highest in the South and Midwest

## 4.3 Patient Experience by Region
```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("visuals/r_plots/eda/patient_experience_by_region.png")
```
- The Northeast shows the strongest patient experience scores

## 4.4 TEC Score by Region
```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("visuals/r_plots/eda/tec_by_region.png")
```
- TEC varies widely across all regions

Summary
The EDA confirms:
- The dataset is clean and modeling‑ready
- Missingness patterns are expected and domain‑consistent
- Quality domains show meaningful variation
- Relationships with readmission are strong and interpretable
- Regional differences highlight targeted improvement opportunities
This completes Step 3 and prepares the project for Step 4: Modeling Dataset Construction.
